California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
April 2, 199o
Wi■liam A. Duva■
Internationa■ Brotherhood of
Painters and Allied Trades
■750 New York Avenue′
 NW
Washington′  D.C.  20008
Dear l{r . Duva I :
You have requested advice on behalf
Brotherhood of Painters and Allied Trades
provisions of the PoIiticaI Reform Act.1
RE:  Your Request for Advice
Our Fi■e No. A-90-■
■5
of the International
concerning the campaign
OUESTION
What reporting obligations does the lnternational Brotherhood
of Painters and A■
lied Trades (PAT)have in ca■
ifornia if it
contributes to California state or 
■ocal candidates or comm■
ttees?
DI SCUSSION
In order to obtain additional information, I called your
of f j-ce on February 26, 1990 | and again on March 7 , 1990. Frank
Burkhardt of your office returned my call on rnarch 19, 1990.
Unfortunately, he was unable to provj.de the information necessary
to answer your question accurately. He advised that I respond to
your letter by establishing the different scenarios under which
PAT may have reporting obligations.
SCENARfO I - Assume that PAT has not made contributions2 in
any amount to Californj.a state or local candidates or committees
prior to January 1, 1990. Also assume that PAT has made or
1 
co.r"rnment Code Sections 81OOO-91o15. All statutory
references are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regTulatj,ons appear at 2 California Code of Regulatj,ons
Section 18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 
A contribution is defined by Section 82015 in part as I'a
payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a paynent of a loan by a third
party, or an enforceable promise to nake a payment." The
definition includes non-monetary contributions and Ioans.
428 J Strcct,Suitc800 o P.().B。、807 0 SticralllcntO C,ヽ95804´0ド07 0 (916)322‐ 56()0


willian A. Duval
Page Two
intends to make contributions to california state and local
candidates and committees totaling ]-ess than $l-o,o00 in 1990.
Under Scenario I, PAT would have no filing obligations in
California for the calendar year 1990. (section 820L3.)
(Note: If PAT makes any contribution to state or 1oca1 candidates
or corumittees in California in 1990, PAT will have reporting
obligations in any subsequent year in lrhich PAT contributes $1,000
or more. (Section 82o13 (a) . )
SCENARIo II - Assume that PAT has not made contributions in
any amolint to california state or local, candidates or committees
prior to January 1, 1990. AIso assurne that PAT has made or
intends to make contributions in California totaling $1o,000 or
more in 1990.
Under Scenario II, 
PAT will have a reporting obligation once
it has contributed $1o,ooo. (Section 82013(c).) Enclosed is the
Infornation Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the
Politi,cal Reform Act. organizations, such as yours, which have
not made contributions in california in previous years but have
contributed $1o,0o0 or more in the current calendar year are
considered I'Major Donor cornmittees. rr Please refer to page 10 of
the enclosed manual for a discussion on the three di.f ferent types
of najor donor committees, and review pages 21 and 22 to deterrnine
when reports must be filed in 1990.
As a brief example, if your organization contributes $10,000
or more prj.or to June 30, 1990, to state candidates or comrnittees,
or candidates and conmittees in rnore than one county, PAT must
file the enclosed Form 461 by JuIy 31, 1990. (Please note that
PAT'S filing obligations in 1991 and subsequent years are the same
as would apply under Scenario III, 
beIow.)
SCENARIo III - Assume PAT has made contributions to
california state or local candidates or committees prior to
January 1, 1990. Afso assume that PAT intends to make
contributions totaling $1,o00 this calendar year.
Under Scenario III, 
PAT nust file the enclosed Forn 410 with
the secretary of State's office within 10 days of contributing
S1,00o in California. 
(section 84101.) The secretary of state's
address is at the top of the form. Upon filing the Form 410, PAT
will be a rrgeneral purpose recipient committee. t' (Section 84101.)
This cornmittee must report its activities 
in California.
(Section 84211.) Uost labor unions are able to use the enclosed
Forn 45O to report their activities. 
The reporting periods and
fi1ing schedule for 1990 is included in the enclosed parnphlet


‘
●
willian A. Duval
Page Three
entitled rrFiling schedules for Elections HeId in 1990-'r In
addition, ttre enclosed document entitled 'rorganizations which
Receive Funds for Purposes other than l'laking contributions to or
Expenditures on Behal,f of california State or Local candidates,
sallot lleasures, or committeesrr wiII assist you in reporting.
If you have any additional questions, please call ne at (916)
322-5662 .
sincerely,
Enc Iosures
Kathryn E.
tical Reform consultant
